vertical_split_selection.sort_order: descending by non_pk_column_count, then table name

fragment_roles: rows where A9 > 8 or is NULL
rows where duration > 48 or is NULL
rows where bank_to = QR + rows where bank_to = IJ + rows where bank_to = GH
rows where bank_to = YZ + rows where bank_to = AB
rows where bank_to = WX + rows where bank_to = ST
rows where bank_to = KL + rows where bank_to = UV
rows where bank_to is NULL or not in {'QR', 'YZ', 'AB', 'WX', 'ST', 'KL', 'UV', 'IJ', 'GH'} (catch-all 'OTHER' bucket)
order.fragment_role_A: rows where bank_to = QR + rows where bank_to = IJ + rows where bank_to = GH
order.fragment_role_B: rows where bank_to = YZ + rows where bank_to = AB

fragment_roles: rows where type = junior + rows where type = gold or is NULL
card.fragment_role_B: rows where type = junior + rows where type = gold or is NULL

fragment_roles: rows where gender = F or is NULL
client.fragment_role_B: rows where gender = F or is NULL

fragment_roles: rows where amount > 6800 or is NULL

justification.cross_local_query_count_interpretation: Distinct cross-local workload queries where this fragment pair (different original tables) co-occurs; not an estimate of single-local queries after relocation.

relocation_engine_debug.pair_weight_meaning: Weights count co-occurrences of two fragments (from different original tables) inside workload queries that already span 2+ locals. They rank relocation candidates; they do not equal the number of queries that will become single-local after a move.
